chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَا يُصَلِّ الرَّجُلُ وَ فِي يَدِهِ خَاتَمُ حَدِيدٍ .
وَ رُوِيَ إِذَا كَانَ الْمِفْتَاحُ فِي غِلَافٍ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The man cannot pray and in his hand is an iron ring’.
And it is reported, ‘(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘When the keys were in a wrapping, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 35. Ali has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah has said, ‘A man does not perform Salah (prayer) wearing an iron ring.’” “It is narrated that if a key is inside cover then it is not an offense.” (Ahadith 1-35 of this chapter are best explained by fatwa.)
